description: COMPLETE emissions file for CEN-Chile (SEN). Carries the full IPCC 2006 defaults (15 fuels from the bundled file) PLUS Chile-specific overrides (sub-bituminous coal, LNG upstream chain, pipeline-NG split, Noracid sulfur cogen). Pass via --emissions-file for CEN bundles.
source: Same IPCC 2006 Vol 2 base values as scripts/gtopt_shared/data/ipcc_emission_factors.json + CEN-Chile project-specific overrides. The Gas-LNG-vs-pipeline split is handled by plexos2gtopt::extract_fuels via the _GN_ name-suffix detection (sets FuelSpec.subtype).

units.co2_upstream: tCO2 / GJ — LNG upstream is non-zero (median 10 kgCO2/GJ = 0.01); pipeline NG keeps IPCC default 0.

notes: ALL Chilean coal plants burn sub-bituminous coal (Andean + Colombian imports — NOT bituminous). The `coal` alias here points at sub-bituminous (0.0961 tCO2/GJ) rather than IPCC `coal_bituminous` default (0.0946) — ~1.5% upward correction.
LNG carries a CEN-specific upstream chain: GNL Quintero + GNL Mejillones both source from international LNG via tanker. co2_upstream = 0.01 (= 10 kgCO2/GJ) is a market-median estimate.
Pipeline natural gas (Gas_*_GN_*) keeps co2_upstream = 0 per IPCC default — domestic Argentine + Magallanes pipelines have minimal upstream beyond extraction.
coal_sub_bituminous.heat_content = 25.8 GJ/t (Chilean operational reality) NOT 18.9 (IPCC sub-bit textbook). Colombian-import coal procurement specs run 24-26 GJ/t.

Otros_Noracid -> sulfur_cogen (mineral-zero CO2): 26 MW cogen at Noracid's Mejillones sulfuric-acid plant, burning elemental sulfur (no carbon -> zero CO2 at burner tip). Other Otros_* fuels stay unmatched until each case is reviewed individually — the bare `otros` prefix is CEN's catch-all and would mis-route real CO2 emitters if blanket-aliased.

fuels.name: diesel
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Gas/Diesel Oil (74100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 43.0 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Gas/Diesel Oil (3 kg CH4/TJ, 0.6 kg N2O/TJ)

fuels.name: fuel_oil
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Residual Fuel Oil (77400 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 40.4 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Residual Fuel Oil (3, 0.6)

fuels.name: natural_gas
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Natural Gas (56100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 2.2 — Natural Gas (1, 0.1). CEN override: distinguishes pipeline gas (this, upstream=0) from LNG (above, upstream=0.01). Combustion identical at burner tip.

fuels.name: lng
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Natural Gas at burner tip (56100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 2.2 — Natural Gas (1, 0.1). CEN override: co2_upstream = 0.01 tCO2/GJ = 10 kgCO2/GJ — market-median LNG chain (liquefaction + tanker + regas).

fuels.name: coal_bituminous
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Other Bituminous Coal (94600 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 25.8 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Other Bituminous Coal (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: coal_sub_bituminous
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Sub-Bituminous Coal combustion factor (96100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 2.2 — Sub-Bit CH4+N2O (1, 1.5). CEN override: heat_content = 25.8 GJ/t (Chilean op reality — Colombian + Andean imports). IPCC textbook for Sub-Bit is 18.9 but Chilean coal procurement runs 24-26 GJ/t — using 18.9 under-counts CO2 by ~25%.

fuels.name: coal_anthracite
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Anthracite (98300 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 26.7 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Anthracite (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: coal_lignite
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Lignite (101000 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 11.9 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Lignite (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: coking_coal
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Coking Coal (94600 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 28.2 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Coking Coal (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: petcoke
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Petroleum Coke (97500 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 32.5 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Petroleum Coke (3, 0.6 — defaults to liquid-fuel row)

fuels.name: lpg
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Liquefied Petroleum Gases (63100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 47.3 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — LPG (1, 0.1)

fuels.name: kerosene
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Other Kerosene (71900 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 43.8 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Other Kerosene (3, 0.6)

fuels.name: naphtha
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Naphtha (73300 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 44.5 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Naphtha (3, 0.6 — defaults to liquid-fuel row)

fuels.name: crude_oil
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Crude Oil (73300 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 42.3 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Crude Oil (3, 0.6)

fuels.name: biomass
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Wood/Wood Waste (112000 kg CO2/TJ gross); biogenic-zero default per AFOLU-sector accounting (combustion CO2 offset by sustainable uptake). Use co2_combustion_gross for a non-zero biomass scenario.; Table 2.2 — Wood/Wood Waste (30 kg CH4/TJ, 4 kg N2O/TJ — NOT biogenic-zero like CO2: combustion byproducts)

fuels.name: biogas
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Other Biogas (54600 kg CO2/TJ gross). Biogenic-zero default; use co2_combustion_gross when biogenic CO2 must be priced.; Table 2.2 — Other Biogas (1, 0.1)

fuels.name: sulfur_cogen
fuels.ipcc_reference: Elemental-sulfur combustion (Noracid SACI, Mejillones — 720 kt/y H2SO4 plant + 26 MW cogen, online 2012; feedstock 240 kt/y elemental sulfur). CO2 = 0: no carbon in feedstock (mineral-zero, distinct from biomass biogenic-zero and geothermal non-combustion). NOT in IPCC AR6 stationary-combustion defaults (IPCC covers HYDROCARBON fuels only). Non-GHG SO2 (H2SO4 process) + NOx (high-temp combustion) defer to EPA AP-42 / EMEP-EEA Guidebook. heat_content = 9.3 GJ/t (elemental sulfur LHV, typical 8.9-9.4 GJ/t). CEN PCP fuel name: `Otros_Noracid`.

fuels.name: geothermal
fuels.ipcc_reference: Geothermal is non-combustion. IPCC stationary-combustion default = 0. Non-condensable-gas releases at specific fields are site-specific and not part of an IPCC default.; Geothermal: non-combustion (0, 0)

generator_overrides._comment: Per-Generator metadata overrides for CEN-Chile units where the PLEXOS bundle / PLP source carries `fuel` + (HR == 0) — i.e. the unit's energy source is non-commercial (waste heat, geothermal steam, pulp-mill black liquor / wood residue) so there is no meaningful per-MWh fuel-cost signal. plexos2gtopt + plp2gtopt's PLEXOS overlay consult this list to (a) drop the spurious `fuel` reference and (b) set the canonical `Generator.type` to `thermal:cogen` / `renewable:geothermal` so the gtopt validation warning ('fuel set but no heat_rate') stops firing on legitimate non-fuel units.

PAS_MEJILLONES.source: CEN-SIP /unidades-generadoras/v4: id_unidad=2018 'TER PLANTA DE ACIDO SULFURICO MEJILLONES U1' (NORACID S.A., 20.07 MW, Cogeneración - TV); curva_consumo = 'No Aplica'
PAS_MEJILLONES.rationale: Steam-turbine on sulfuric-acid plant waste heat. The 'fuel' is elemental sulfur burned in the acid process, not a commercial generation fuel; per-MWh HR is undefined.

CMPC_BUCALEMU_2.source: CEN-SIP family pattern (CMPC pulp-mill cogens): CMPC SANTA FE U1/U2, CMPC PACIFICO U1/U2, CMPC CORDILLERA U1 all registered as Cogeneración-TV / Termoeléctrica TV with curva_consumo pointing at private 'Informe CEN' docs (no public kJ/kWh value).
CMPC_BUCALEMU_2.rationale: CMPC Bucalemu pulp-mill cogen unit 2 — burns black liquor + wood residue from the pulping process; no commercial-fuel HR by CEN convention.

CERRO_DOMINADOR_CS.rationale: Cerro Dominador concentrated-solar plant with molten-salt storage; solar resource is the primary energy, no fuel consumed at the LP boundary.

CERRO_PABELLON_U1.rationale: Cerro Pabellón geothermal plant (Enel Green Power), unit 1.
